facts.source: Manual Azure deployment verification and earlier repository documentation
facts.notes: Retained as historical evidence of a previously verified working size. Newer Azure control-plane evidence shows the current VM is Standard_B2ats_v2.

facts.source: Manual collector verification recorded in repository documentation
facts.notes: The read-only replacement planner did not execute guest commands, so it does not re-prove the currently running ServiceTracer version or service health.

facts.value: Ubuntu 24.04 with Python 3.11+ gate, deterministic certificate identity, and a shared desired-image contract
facts.notes: The desired image differs from the deployed Ubuntu 22.04 Jammy image line and requires replacement rather than an ordinary in-place VM update.

facts.value: Managed-identity publication to a dedicated Azure Storage static website endpoint
facts.notes: The replacement-plan artifact found no visible role assignments for the current collector identity. Publication access must be explicitly granted to the replacement identity when that feature is deployed.

facts.value: Two private Ubuntu backend VMs behind the Standard Public Load Balancer
facts.notes: Live Azure proof remains a separately governed lifecycle operation.

facts.value: Read-only What-If with bounded VM candidate planning, ARM validation, pre-planning image-drift detection, and a separate collector replacement planner
facts.source: Merged pull requests 15 through 20 and collector replacement plan run 29856203054
facts.notes: The planner run completed successfully with Azure mutations unauthorized and unperformed.

facts.value: The deployed Canonical Ubuntu 22.04 Jammy collector image line differs from the desired Canonical Ubuntu 24.04 image line
facts.source: Collector replacement plan run 29856203054, artifact SHA-256 76f3b44e6e97e906dac6d62eeb212a6bc55265e77271a030b9c45b0cacf55637
facts.notes: Ordinary in-place deployment remains blocked. This finding authorizes planning only, not replacement execution.

facts.value: 32 GiB Standard SSD evidence disk is attached, uses deleteOption Detach, disables public network access, and denies network access
facts.notes: A separately verified snapshot or backup is required before any future replacement execution. Guest mount and evidence readability still require pre-maintenance verification.

facts.value: Collector NIC uses a static private address on the operations subnet and is configured with VM deleteOption Delete
facts.notes: Any replacement design must either preserve the NIC or deterministically recreate and verify its subnet, static addressing, and security relationships. Exact network identifiers remain in the protected workflow artifact.

facts.value: Collector uses a system-assigned managed identity; the planner observed no visible role assignments for that principal
facts.notes: A replacement VM will receive a new principal identity. Required publication permissions must be explicitly recreated and verified.

facts.value: Current disposable OS disk allows public network access and uses AllowAll network access policy
facts.notes: The replacement design should disable public disk access unless a documented requirement and compensating controls are approved.

facts.value: Read-only inventory and replacement assessment with evidence-disk, NIC, identity, RBAC, and recovery boundaries
facts.notes: Evidence quality review passed for control-plane inventory. Replacement authorization remains withheld pending guest verification, recovery-point proof, NIC handling, security review, cost controls, and explicit human approval.
